rationale.R1.1: The broad request invites assessment of the passage's thesis, gap framing, and contribution claim.
rationale.R1.2: Evaluating what one thinks of the passage centrally includes checking whether its argument about liveness, metrics, and conclusions coheres.
rationale.R1.3: The passage makes empirical claims from benchmark results, so support between reported measurements and interpretations is central.
rationale.R1.4: The artifact explicitly claims first standardization and extension beyond prior work, making novelty assessment central.
rationale.R1.7: The passage relies on named prior work and attribution, so scholarship and citation responsibility is central.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of broad feedback should disclose uncertainty about unstated paper context and unavailable sources.
rationale.RX.2: The response must avoid overstating what can be inferred from the excerpt alone.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within projecting responsibilities rather than performing the requested assessment.
rationale.RX.4: The projection should avoid enabling misleading research claims or unsupported scholarly positioning.
rationale.RX.5: The rationale should trace active responsibilities to specific features of the delegation and artifact.
